title: A nation divides
content: In 1861, a group of southern states broke away to form the Confederacy. The remaining states — the Union — refused to let the country split apart.

title: The fault line
content: At the heart of the divide was slavery and the question of whether it would expand. Economic and political differences between North and South deepened the rift.

title: A turning point
content: After two years of war, key battles in 1863 shifted momentum toward the Union and reshaped the conflict's purpose.

title: The war ends
content: By 1865 the Confederacy could no longer continue. The Union was preserved, and slavery was abolished — but rebuilding the nation would take generations.
